worked at Vintage fof in Israel, moved to SF with them a few years ago

stage funds, sub-100m, like sub-50 as well

foundations/endowments outsource to them, looking for endowment style
portfolio
Who they like: they backed Browder, Basecase's recent fund. He thought david
was smart, but that "his roommate is smarter" (I guess he means rohan).
Thought Koko Carlo are smart but lacked track record, hence passed
Asked him why basecase / what's her edge? He says references from
great founders was super impressive, she's in a super competitive space
with the types of founders she backs and yet has established a strong
enough brand that competes with larger funds, embedded in the right
networks, goes super deep very early with a small set of founders and
they take a liking to her
They need to see some sort of track record, like to see consistency in
investments, don't like sudden change of mandate/scope
European exposure at GEM is only Index. At Vintage they had a great
european portfolio: point9, blueyard, etc. He doesn't know Fly
We mostly met to talk about India - he's now looking there increasingly
He's met the big funds (peak xv, lightspeed etc) and has met the super
small ones like Gradcap, campus fund - they feel lost because they
haven't done the benchmarking regionally
have not talked to any of the middle category like Prime, 3one4, Blume
types
gave him a lay of the land, told him he should talk to Prime, Sauce,
Northpoint, Together (for India to world SaaS pov), Roel, gradcap (he knew

name: Everblue Capital Management
Dziugas
call_notes:
$55m for VC
20m for directs, Lithuania
35m for smaller funds
Early summer discussion he can do
He wants to hire someone
He really wants to do it
90% chance committing to us
He wants to come back
After hiring
After polishing
Not 2m and not 20m check
@December 15, 2025
He came early in the weekend to see Chelsea game and City game, he's apparently Arsenal fan and also likes basketball, supports Kaunas from Lithuania. He was preoccupied with their exit event, says they're looking for 10x and two bidders offer 9.4x, small margin but needs to close in a month or so.
The current management of their company is mostly family, his dad and uncle, CEO joined right after his dad owns 10% but probably only outside management, 90% belongs to his dad / family and they are looking to exit 70% or so. It's basically a family business, seems traditional and Dziugas seems to be bridging the professional sales process of IB guys they hired and his family, sounds a bit stuck and he's usually not really involved on the operations side. He said they're also looking what's next for his dad, 56 yo guy and family office side isn't really operational and hands on enough, ideally looking to get more involved with some business ideas—I gave example of Alleycorp to him. They sound liking involvement and said also asked GP stage question because of it. I drew boundary around we like involved LPs but no outside control whatsoever.
He prior wrote $5m each into Isomer (he won't re-up), Vintage (he's happy) and also Truebridge (2-3 months ago). He is very happy to pay FoF fees and gets that screening 100s of funds isn't for him and he'd better have professional people do it. He wants to do 1-2 more FoF for that with $5-10m checks, closer to $10m if exit happens. He says he wants to be involved and meet funds himself, asked how realistic he moves into some of funds we did and what % we can get access for him. For directs he sounds more interested in his local network first, so not likely to move with us in the US, etc.
He says next 6-8 weeks he's preoccupied with the exit, even in the lunch bankers calling him all the time etc. his head is there, but he says he sees a world he writes a $5-10m check in the new year and him and Vineyard works closely. I think he also sees Hummingbird as a great brand and when he saw us leaving he took the opportunity as he couldn't invest prior—but he clearly sees us as the continuation. He is close to Willgrow, but says Justinas can be annoying at times very 'this fund that fund' focused.
We agreed to reconnect end of Jan when he has more bandwidth and Jordan is in London. In the meantime I said we would send materials his way. I said we can get him in writing a working doc outlining how we could work together. Let's do that in Jan, basically outlining below,
We don't like control but like involvement from LPs, better for our GPs.
Idk your relation with other FoFs you did but even in hummingbird you would interact with CFO-IR, etc. two layers of people, now coming into a new fund as first believer, you just call me.
We are yong guys building it in London close to you, same mentality as you, not suited up or uptight Yankees other side of the world.
We saw value of being first believer in our GPs, and we also value first-believers. If you write $10m check in our fund as anchor, you matter to us, not same as joining a $2-3b FoF.
We're just a few partners making investments, same for our funds, you just call me I call GP—it's a direct relationship, no intermediaries or email chains.
Basically he's a young dude vibing well with us, we just need to give vibes that we're same vibes and if you want involvement don't go to 50 yo suited up dudes + big platforms—come back us instead.

name: Weizmann Institute endowment
call_notes:
Ben Kiley - W-GEM Background & Mandate
Works for Weizmann Institute endowment ($5B AUM)
Based in The Hague for 1 year (wife's government role), normally NYC
Portfolio strategy: <45 relationships across entire $5B portfolio
Extremely concentrated, long-term approach
Go early in manager lifecycle (Fund 1-2s increasingly preferred)
No geographic quotas - purely bottom-up opportunity assessment
Substantial backing when they commit
Current India exposure: Z47 (ex-Matrix India), 2 public managers, 1 legacy manager
Z47 relationship less compelling post-Matrix separation
India Venture Assessment & Targets
Market challenges identified:
Extremely competitive, potentially more than Silicon Valley for software/AI
High valuations across ecosystem
Risk of 3x net returns with delayed DPI - not compelling for W-GEM
Fraud risk concerns (standard India considerations)
Manager categories discussed:
Large established (Sequoia, Nexus) - overpriced deals, signal not strong
Mid-tier proven (Bloom, 314, Prime) - fund size inflation concerns
Emerging differentiated - preferred target

name: Jordan park
call_notes:
He was overall warm on the strategy as he doesn't think he'd spend a ton of time with folks we invest in. They do internal FoF products themselves he runs, didn't add many funds recently, but also their check sizes grew as ppl they backed have scaled, so spending less time on net new / smaller funds. He's doing both funds and co-invests and spending time across VC + PE.
The team was heading a wealth management division in Goldman and took that with them. It's a large team, he's heading venture but has a guy above him. $9b managing, $1b venture book. 20 or so relationships. He said he really likes Compound but min check size is $15m for them. Couldn't align timing with Mike. He's interested in doing Fund 2 / 3s.
Has legacy relationships in China, didn't do much outside of US but overall curious but low bandwidth.
